name: kitops

metadata.name: kitops
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A package for working with KitOps' ModelKits

documentation:
# kitops
Open-source Python package for [KitOps'](https://kitops.ml) ModelKit management

## Installation:
```bash
pip install kitops
```
## Documentation:
[Python KitOps Docs](https://jozu-ai.github.io/pykitops/)

## Contribute To The Project
[pykitops on GitHub](https://github.com/jozu-ai/pykitops)

functions.name: kitops.modelkit.utils.validate_dict
documentation:
Validate a dictionary against allowed keys.

Examples:
    >>> validate_dict({"a": 1, "b": 2}, {"a", "b"})
    None

    >>> validate_dict({"a": 1, "b": 2}, {"a"})
    ValueError: Found unallowed key(s): b

    >>> validate_dict({"a": 1, "d": 2}, {"a", "b", "c"})
    ValueError: Found unallowed key(s): d

Args:
    value (Dict[str, Any]): Value to validate.
    allowed_keys (Set[str]): Set of allowed keys.

functions.name: kitops.modelkit.utils.clean_empty_items
documentation:
Remove empty items from a dictionary or list.

Examples:
    >>> clean_empty_items({"a": "", "b": "c", "d": None})
    {'b': 'c'}

    >>> clean_empty_items(["", "a", None])
    ['a']

Args:
    d (Any): Dictionary or list to clean.

Returns:
    Any: Cleaned dictionary or list.

functions.name: kitops.modelkit.utils.get_or_create_directory
documentation:
Get or create a directory.

Examples:
    >>> get_or_create_directory("my-directory")
    "my-directory"

Args:
    directory (str): Directory to get or create.

Returns:
    str: The directory.

functions.name: kitops.modelkit.utils.parse_modelkit_tag
documentation:
Parse a ModelKit tag into its components.

Examples:
    >>> parse_modelkit_tag("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
    {
        "registry": "jozu.ml",
        "namespace": "jozu-demos",
        "model": "titanic-survivability",
        "tag": "latest"
    }

Args:
    tag (str): Tag to parse.

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: Parsed components of the tag.

classes.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ModelKitManager instance.

Args:
    working_directory (str): The working directory. 
        Defaults to os.getcwd(). This is the directory used by
        the ModelKitManager to work with the given ModelKit or 
        used to create a new ModelKit. The ModelKit's Kitfile
        is also read from and saved to this directory. If the 
        given directory does not exist, it will be created, if possible;
        otherwise, an error will be raised.
    user_credentials (Optional[UserCredentials]): The user credentials. Defaults to None.
        If None, the user credentials are loaded from environment variables.
    modelkit_reference (Optional[ModelKitReference]): The modelkit reference. Defaults to None.
        If None, the modelkit reference is created from the modelkit tag.
    modelkit_tag (Optional[str]): The modelkit tag to parse into a ModelKitReference. Defaults to None.
        If None, an empty ModelKitReference is created.

Examples:
    >>> manager = ModelKitManager()
    >>> manager.working_directory
    <WorkingDirectory>
    >>> manager.user_credentials
    <UserCredentials>
    >>> manager.modelkit_reference
    <ModelKitReference>

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.working_directory
documentation:
Gets the working directory.

Returns:
    str: The working directory.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.working_directory
documentation:
Sets the working directory.

Args:
    value (str): The working directory to set.

Gets the user credentials.

Returns:

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.user_credentials

Sets the user credentials.

Args:

Sets the Kitfile.

Args:
    value (Kitfile): The Kitfile to set.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.pull_and_unpack_modelkit
documentation:
Unpacks the ModelKit into the working directory.

Args:
    filters (Optional[list[str]]): The filters to apply when 
        unpacking the ModelKit.  Defaults to None.  The filters
        are used to specify the Kitfile parts to unpack from the
        ModelKit (e.g. ["model", "data"]). If None, all parts
        are unpacked.
    load_kitfile (bool): If True, the Kitfile will be loaded 
        from the working directory afer the ModelKit has been
        unpacked. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.pack_and_push_modelkit
documentation:
Packs the ModelKit from the working directory and pushes it 
to the registry.

Args:
    save_kitfile (bool): If True, the Kitfile will be saved to 
        the working directory before the Kitfile is packed and
        pushed. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.manager.ModelKitManager.remove_modelkit
documentation:
Removes the ModelKit from the registry.

Args:
    local (Optional[bool]): If True, the ModelKit will be removed
        from the local registry. Defaults to True.
    remote (Optional[bool]): If True, the ModelKit will be removed 
        from the remote registry.

Returns:
    None

Examples:
    >>> modelkit_tag = "jozu.ml/brett/titanic-survivability:latest"
    >>> manager = ModelKitManager(working_directory = "temp/titanic-full",
    ...                           modelkit_tag = modelkit_tag)
    >>> manager.remove_modelkit(local = True, remote = True)

A class to represent a modelkit manager.
This class manages the user credentials and modelkit reference.
Attributes:
    user_credentials (UserCredentials): The user credentials.
    modelkit_reference (ModelKitReference): The modelkit reference.
    Methods:
    __init__():
        Initializes the ModelKitManager instance.
    working_directory:
        Gets or sets the working directory.
    user_credentials:
        Gets or sets the user credentials.
    modelkit_reference:
        Gets or sets the modelkit reference.

classes.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the ModelKitReference instance by parsing a tag.

Args:
    modelkit_tag (Optional[str]): The tag to parse. It should be in the form of:
        {registry}/{namespace}/{model}:{tag}
    
Examples:
    >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
    >>> ref.registry
    'jozu.ml'
    >>> ref.namespace
    'jozu-demos'
    >>> ref.repository
    'titanic-survivability'
    >>> ref.tag
    'latest'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.registry
documentation: Gets the registry.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.registry
documentation:
Sets the registry.

Args:
    value (str): The registry to set.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the registry is not a string or is not None

Examples:
    >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
    >>> ref.registry = "new_registry"
    >>> ref.registry
    'new_registry'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.namespace
documentation:
Gets the namespace.

Examples:
    >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
    >>> ref.namespace = "new_namespace"
    >>> ref.namespace
    'new_namespace'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.namespace
documentation:
Sets the namespace.

Args:
    value (str): The namespace to set.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the namespace is not a string or is not None.
        
        Examples:
        >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
        >>> ref.namespace = "new_namespace"
        >>> ref.namespace
        'new_namespace'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.repository
documentation:
Gets the repository name.

Examples:
    >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
    >>> ref.repository = "new_model"
    >>> ref.repository
    'new_repository'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.repository
documentation:
Sets the repository name.

Args:
    value (str): The repository name to set.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the repository name is not a string or is None.
        
    Examples:
        >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
        >>> ref.repository = "new_repository"
        >>> ref.repository
        'new_repository'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.tag
documentation:
Gets the tag.

Examples:
    >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
    >>> ref.tag = "new_tag"
    >>> ref.tag
    'new_tag'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.reference.ModelKitReference.tag
documentation:
Sets the tag.

Args:
    value (str): The tag to set.
    
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the tag is not a string or None.
        
    Examples:
        >>> ref = ModelKitReference("jozu.ml/jozu-demos/titanic-survivability:latest")
        >>> ref.tag = "new_tag"
        >>> ref.tag
        'new_tag'

A class to represent a modelkit reference broken down into its parts.
This class parses a modelkit tag and provides access to its components.
These include the registry, namespace, model, and tag.

Attributes:
    registry (str): The registry for the model.
    namespace (str): The namespace for the model.
    model (str): The model name.
    tag (str): The tag for the model.

Methods:
    __init__():
        Initializes the ModelKitReference instance by parsing a tag.
    registry:
        Gets or sets the registry.
    namespace:
        Gets or sets the namespace.
    repository:
        Gets or sets the repository name.
    tag:
        Gets or sets the tag.

classes.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the UserCredentials instance with the values provided.
If no values are provided, they are loaded from environment variables.
Initializes the private attributes _username, _password, _registry, and _namespace.
Raises:
    ValueError: If either username or password is missing from the provided arguments or environment variables.

    Examples:
    >>> user = UserCredentials()
    >>> user.username
    'user'
    >>> user.password
    'password'
    >>> user.registry
    'registry'
    >>> user.namespace
    'namespace'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.username
documentation:
Gets the username.

    Examples:
    >>> user = UserCredentials()
    >>> user.username
    'user'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.username
documentation:
Sets the username.
Args:
    value (str): The username to set.
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the username is not a string.
        
        Examples:
        >>> user = UserCredentials()
        >>> user.username = 'new_user'
        >>> user.username
        'new_user'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.password
documentation:
Gets the password.

    Examples:
    >>> user = UserCredentials()
    >>> user.password = 'new_password'
    >>> user.password
    'new_password'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.password
documentation:
Sets the password.
Args:
    value (str): The password to set.
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the password is not a string.

        Examples:
        >>> user = UserCredentials()
        >>> user.password = 'new_password'
        >>> user.password
        'new_password'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.registry
documentation:
Gets the registry information.

    Examples:
    >>> user = UserCredentials()
    >>> user.registry = 'new_registry'
    >>> user.registry
    'new_registry'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.registry
documentation:
Sets the registry information.

Args:
    value (str | None): The registry information to set.
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the registry information is not a string
            or None.

        Examples:
        >>> user = UserCredentials()
        >>> user.registry = 'new_registry'
        >>> user.registry
        'new_registry'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.namespace
documentation:
Gets the namespace information.

    Examples:
    >>> user = UserCredentials()
    >>> user.namespace = 'new_namespace'
    >>> user.namespace
    'new_namespace'

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.user.UserCredentials.namespace
documentation:
Sets the namespace information.

Args:
    value (str | None): The namespace information to set.
    Raises:
        ValueError: If the namespace information is not a string.

        Examples:
        >>> user = UserCredentials()
        >>> user.namespace = 'new_namespace'
        >>> user.namespace
        'new_namespace'

A class to manage user credentials loaded from environment variables.
Attributes:
    username (str): The username for the user.
    password (str): The password for the user.
    registry (Optional[str]): The registry information for the user.
    namespace (Optional[str]): The namespace information for the user.
Methods:
    __init__():
        Initializes the UserCredentials instance by loading environment variables.
    username:
        Gets or sets the username.
    password:
        Gets or sets the password.
    registry:
        Gets or sets the registry information.
    namespace:
        Gets or sets the namespace information.

classes.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Kitfile from a path to an existing Kitfile, or 
create an empty Kitfile.

Examples:
    >>> kitfile = Kitfile(path="path/to/Kitfile")
    >>> kitfile.to_yaml()

    >>> kitfile = Kitfile()
    >>> kitfile.manifestVersion = "1.0"
    >>> kitfile.package = {"name": "my_package", "version": "0.1.0",
    ...                    "description": "My package description",
    ...                    "authors": ["Author 1", "Author 2"]}
    >>> kitfile.code = [{"path": "code/", "description": "Code description",
    ...                  "license": "Apache-2.0"}]
    >>> kitfile.datasets = [{"name": "my_dataset", "path": "datasets/",
    ...                      "description": "Dataset description",
    ...                      "license": "Apache-2.0"}]
    >>> kitfile.docs = [{"path": "docs/", "description": "Docs description"}]
    >>> kitfile.model = {"name": "my_model", "path": "model/",
    ...                  "framework": "tensorflow", "version": "2.0.0",
    ...                  "description": "Model description",
    ...                  "license": "Apache-2.0", "parts": [],
    ...                  "parameters": ""}
    >>> kitfile.to_yaml()
    'manifestVersion: 1.0
     package:
         name: my_package
         version: 0.1.0
         description: My package description
         authors:
         - Author 1
         - Author 2
     code:
     - path: code/
       description: Code description
       license: Apache-2.0
     datasets:
     - name: my_dataset
       path: datasets/
       description: Dataset description
       license: Apache-2.0
     docs:
     - path: docs/
       description: Docs description
     model:
         name: my_model
         path: model/
         framework: tensorflow
         version: 2.0.0
         description: Model description
         license: Apache-2.0'

Args:
    path (str, optional): Path to existing Kitfile to load. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Kitfile (Kitfile): Kitfile object.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile._initialize_kitfile_section_validators
documentation: Initialize validators for Kitfile sections.

documentation:
Set the datasets section.

Args:
    value (List[Dict[str, Any]]): Datasets section.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.docs
documentation:
Get the docs section.

Returns:
    List[Dict[str, Any]]: Docs section.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.docs
documentation:
Set the docs section.

Args:
    value (List[Dict[str, Any]]): Docs section.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.model
documentation:
Get the model section.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: Model section.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.model
documentation:
Set the model section.

Args:
    value (Dict[str, Any]): Model section.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.to_yaml
documentation:
Serialize the Kitfile to YAML format. 

Args:
    suppress_empty_values (bool, optional): Whether to suppress 
        empty values. Defaults to True.
Returns:
    str: YAML representation of the Kitfile.

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.print
documentation:
Print the Kitfile to the console.

Returns:
    None

methods.name: kitops.modelkit.kitfile.Kitfile.save
documentation:
Save the Kitfile to a file.

Args:
    path (str): Path to save the Kitfile. Defaults to "Kitfile".
    print (bool): If True, print the Kitfile to the console. 
        Defaults to True.

Returns:
    None

Examples:
    >>> kitfile = Kitfile()
    >>> kitfile.save("path/to/Kitfile")

Kitfile class to manage KitOps ModelKits and Kitfiles.

Attributes:
    path (str): Path to the Kitfile.

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.info
documentation:
Retrieve information about a kit repository.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository along with the tag.
    filters (Optional[List[str]]): A list of kitfile parts for which to
        retrieve information. Defaults to None.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None


Examples:
    >>> info("jozu.ml/brett/titanic-survivability:latest")
    # Returns information from the local registry about the 
    # "titanic-survivability:latest" ModelKit.

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.inspect
documentation:
Inspect a repository using the 'kit' command.

Parameters:
repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository along with the tag.
remote (Optional[bool]): Flag to indicate if the inspection should be done remotely. Defaults to True.
    Otherwise, the inspection will be done locally.
**kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.list
documentation:
Lists the ModelKits available in the specified repository path.

Args:
    repo_path_without_tag (Optional[str]): The path to the repository without the tag. 
                                           If not provided, lists kits from the local registry.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.login
documentation:
Logs in to the specified registry using the provided username and password.

Args:
    user (str): The username for the registry.
    passwd (str): The password for the registry.
    registry (str, optional): The registry URL. Defaults to "jozu.ml".
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.logout
documentation:
Logs out from the specified registry.

Args:
    registry (str, optional): The registry to log out from. Defaults to "jozu.ml".
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.pack
documentation:
Packs the current directory into a ModelKit package with a specified tag.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The repository path along with the tag to be used for the package.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.pull
documentation:
Pulls the specified ModelKit from the remote registry.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository along with the tag to pull.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.push
documentation:
Pushes the specified ModelKit to the remote registry.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository along with the tag to be pushed.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.remove
documentation:
Remove a ModelKit from the registry.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository with its tag.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.tag
documentation:
Tag a ModelKit with a new tag.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository with its tag.
    repo_path_with_new_tag (str): The new tag to be assigned to the ModelKit.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    subprocess.CalledProcessError: If the command returns a non-zero exit status

Examples:
    >>> tag("jozu.ml/brett/titanic-survivability:latest", 
            "jozu.ml/brett/titanic-survivability:v2")

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.unpack
documentation:
Unpacks a ModelKit to the specified directory from the remote registry.

This function constructs a command to unpack a ModelKit and 
calls an internal function to execute the command.

Args:
    repo_path_with_tag (str): The path to the repository along with 
        the tag to be unpacked.
    dir (str): The directory to unpack the ModelKit to.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit.version
documentation:
Lists the version of the KitOps Command-line Interface (CLI).

Args:
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: kitops.cli.kit._run
documentation:
Executes a command in the system shell.

Args:
    command (List[Any]): The command to be executed as a list of strings.
    input (Optional[str]): Optional input to be passed to the command.
    verbose (bool): If True, print the command before executing. Defaults to True.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments to pass to the command.
    
Returns:
    None

Raises:
    subprocess.CalledProcessError: If the command returns a non-zero exit status.

functions.name: kitops.cli.utils._process_flag
documentation:
Processes the flag specified by key_full_name, it it exists

Args:
    **kwargs: The arguments from which to extract the specified flag to be 
    processed, if found.

Returns:
    List[str]: The processed flag, if found; otherwise, an empty list.
